recentTweets.text: Very fun night at the St Agnes Carnival! https://t.co/OUQnh9pbok

Today we celebrated 8 years of UP for Women and Children — the sole day shelter exclusively serving women experiencing homelessness and their children here in Louisville.

Words can't express how grateful we are for all they do. https://t.co/06XbdLEeQQ

All of Donald Trump's schemes are different, but the pattern is the same.

He turns public office into private profit every chance he gets. https://t.co/XilyzTCbc7

Trump lost the 2020 election and 6 years later he still can’t let it go.

Don’t mistake his delusions for facts.

Undermining trust in our elections is directly out of the authoritarian playbook.
Every Republican just voted against my amendment to block taxpayer money for Trump's ballroom. https://t.co/8ckMFmuZbr

When Republicans in Congress show you who they are, believe them.

Their budget does nothing to make your life easier, because all they do here is Donald Trump's bidding. https://t.co/1J8y7dHISz

RT @HouseBudgetDems: .@RepMcGarvey introduced an amendment to rescind funding for Trump's ballroom.
 
But Republicans would rather triple d…

Republicans will use every word in the book to avoid admitting their budget puts our country into more debt.

But it does — and doesn't do anything to make gas, groceries, or going to the doctor cheaper. https://t.co/zq2wlD0Tl0

In less than a week, ICE agents shot and killed two people.

There's been no accountability, no investigation, and no justice.

And now Trump is going out of his way to enable them.

It's always a good week when @steelworkers are in DC — including some of our brothers and sisters from USW District 8!

Steelworkers power this country, and we're standing together in the fight together to protect every worker's health and safety. https://t.co/rZ5V0KZHtG

My heart is heavy for the families of Joan Sebastian Guerrero and Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and their entire communities in Maine and Houston.

We will not stop fighting for the justice and accountability they deserve.

In less than a week, ICE has shot and killed two people.

The Trump administration will try to explain this away, but there is no excuse.

ICE continues to terrorize our communities and kill people with no accountability — and this administration goes above and beyond to enable

Trump's mass detention campaign is illegal, unconstitutional, and cruel.

And in 15,000 rulings, 464 different federal judges agree. https://t.co/GKBVF2hKTl

The Camp Ground Rd data center is going to increase the demand on our power grid.

We cannot allow out-of-state companies to continue exploiting our people and resources for their bottom line.

Louisvillians shouldn’t be the ones to pay for it. https://t.co/n3zjgQSULn
Read more: https://t.co/2ZTDutqXPt

Blatantly racist and wrong.

This is the foundation of his immigration policy. This is what billions of our taxpayer dollars are funding.

Affordable health care was already out of reach for so many Americans.

Republicans in Congress made it worse.

They picked massive handouts for their billionaire donors over making sure families can go to the doctor. https://t.co/TAGOcQrOyc
Read more: https://t.co/nns6JZ49cT

I just conducted an oversight visit at an ICE detention facility in Kentucky.

The stories I heard are heartbreaking. They're tearing families apart and spending billions of taxpayer dollars to do it.

This cannot be reformed. Abolish ICE. https://t.co/FC1vjawPLq

Trump made over $1B from crypto last year.

Now he's is openly bragging that he won’t investigate crypto-related crimes.

He will always put his own interests above anything else.

DACA recipients have done everything right, but the Trump admin continues to punish them.

Extreme renewal processing delays mean Louisvillians are losing jobs and struggling to provide for their families — through no fault of their own.

We're demanding answers. https://t.co/bBDZ9vPZP0
Happy Fourth, Louisville! 🇺🇸 https://t.co/gkHhSLw5Pp
And he wants you to pay for his tacky ballroom.
The Trump Administration doesn't get to decide who's worthy to live here — if you were born in America, you're an American. Period. https://t.co/Pxx6Fu49WT
Trump's war with Iran is hurting everyone — even his own administration can't deny it.

If you’re born in the United States, you’re a citizen. 

Period. 

The Trump administration is going after the legal status of anyone they don’t deem worthy to live in this country, and the Supreme Court should’ve never entertained it for this long.

We can’t continue to let billionaire donors spend limitless money in our elections and keep calling it democracy. 

Everyone agrees our system is broken in America, and today the Supreme Court made it a whole lot worse.

The Trump administration has gone out of its way to make life even harder for a couple of kids in Kentucky who already have it hard enough. 

Meanwhile, 15,000 Kentuckians have already lost Medicaid this year because of how much costs have gone up.

Republicans are hoping they

Our democracy is strongest when every eligible vote is counted, no matter how they're cast.

Trump is hell-bent on making it harder to vote, but today SCOTUS ruled against his efforts to undermine fair elections — a win for every American's right to have their voice heard.

Yesterday, our community lost one of our finest in Kenny Klein, a giant in college athletics nationwide and a legend in Louisville sports after an incredible nearly 4 decade career at UofL. 

He’ll long be remembered in Louisville history, and our hearts are with his entire

The Congressional App Challenge is open to all Louisville middle and high schoolers regardless of coding experience—it's a great way to develop STEM skills and make a difference in our community! 

Visit my website to learn more and apply by October 26: https://t.co/TJfHH3RKJ1 https://t.co/UsM0lb4C8C

Too many deafblind students aren’t getting the support they need to get the same education as their peers.

This DeafBlind Awareness Week, I'm working to change that. My bill would close that gap for deaf, blind, and deafblind students. 
https://t.co/3jqDNb2nPk

This has nothing to do with legal status and it never has. 

The Trump administration is going after immigrants with legal status just so they can deport more people — and apparently the Supreme Court is going to let them do it.

If you still think this isn’t racist, look no

Yesterday, Trump blocked a bill to build more affordable housing.

Apparently housing in this country is "of minor importance” but his DC vanity projects are a top priority.

Here’s how much they’re costing us…
https://t.co/vNfBipp46Q https://t.co/fzNghLGUKl

Miscarriage is incredibly common, and for many of our neighbors it's the worst situation imaginable. 

No one should have to face the threat of arrest on top of that pain.

As we mark 4 years without Roe, I'm leading an effort to end pregnancy criminalization. https://t.co/EAH3Ns1vD0

In the 4 years since the Dobbs decision, Kentuckians have suffered under one of the most extreme abortion bans in the country. 

The consequences have been devastating. 

Congress must restore the right to safe, legal abortion. https://t.co/1r5qb1Vgnl

$500/month goes a long way toward staying on top of rent and keeping up with the cost of groceries and gas — especially when you're starting out.

My Young Adult Tax credit is the starting block young Americans deserve to have a fair shot at success. https://t.co/0JA33wYO8v

WE'RE HIRING! The Green & Gold Congressional Aide Program is an incredible opportunity to support and learn from our veterans, and we're honored to participate.

Interested veterans, active-duty spouses, & Gold Star Families can find info on my website: https://t.co/1p2POLcKkm https://t.co/ZM6kXlUzC5
Happy Father’s Day to all the Louisville dads and father figures! Being a dad is one of the greatest joys of my life — and truly never boring with these kids 😂 https://t.co/30RkJG7GXC

Juneteenth marks a pivotal moment in our history. 

It's a celebration of freedom and a recognition of those who fought — and continue to fight — for true equality.

We must continue to stand together in that fight, especially as the right to fair representation is under attack.

Thanks to JenCare for hosting us at their second annual Juneteenth celebration.

Freedom has never come without a struggle and we must do everything in our power to build a country where justice, dignity, and opportunity are realized for all. https://t.co/DHIHgxyKeo

The deadline to submit your application for a fall internship with my DC office is June 22! 

Learn more and apply here: https://t.co/KS3sX6FkMZ https://t.co/tleZzmyIrg

Remember when Trump said his ballroom would be 100% privately funded?

He lied and spent over $300 million of our tax dollars on it. And Republicans want to give him $1 billion more. https://t.co/4t7ST1FU0c

Fourteen years ago, America invested in the potential of Dreamers. Since then, DACA recipients have built careers, raised families, paid taxes, and are integral members of our communities.

After 14 years of success, Dreamers deserve permanent protections—not uncertainty.

Today, St. Stephen Baptist Church marks 100 years — a century of faith, service, and leadership.

An incredible congregation, an incredible pastor, and an incredible legacy.

Here's to another century! https://t.co/qPQqYa1xXy

A man who took billions in government subsidies and pays less in taxes than you just became the first trillionaire. 

And 1 in 3 Americans can't afford to go to the doctor.

Part of the reason I got into politics was the experience we went through with our kids in the hospital.

We can always do more when it comes to pediatric care, and I'm so inspired by Nolan's bravery to speak up and advocate for change — at 6 years old! https://t.co/9OAKTHqHbG

Always great to have my family out in DC for the Congressional Baseball Game for charity (and very honored to be the Dem MVP!) 

Sadly no W for the Dems this year, but I think next year might be our year 👀 https://t.co/ShN7QdFl07

Too many communities have been forever changed by gun violence — including Louisville.

We can and must do more to prevent these tragedies. https://t.co/LY0PC99f7w

My amendments would have blocked funding for his special interest projects and put that money toward what our communities actually need. https://t.co/2pfkyYqbKM

There's so much good we could do with $70 billion. 

Just about anything would be better than giving it to ICE. https://t.co/qhhnkbkJXr

Republicans want another $70 billion for ICE. If we put that funding toward housing, we could get a roof over every veteran's head and probably end homelessness altogether.

That's what my amendment would do. https://t.co/8TzV2gxH85

This weekend, I joined our community for a Walk to Prevent Gun Violence. 

We wear orange to honor those we've lost to gun violence and as a symbol of our commitment to doing the work to end this epidemic once and for all. https://t.co/2S5Pj1pRIj

Today, on what would have been Breonna Taylor's 33rd birthday, my heart is with her family and loved ones. 

Breonna was caring, ambitious, and funny — she brought people together, she took care of her community, and she touched so many lives.

 She should be alive to celebrate https://t.co/Zo5lTuq9Fz
